title: Moist Tender Vietnamese Salt Cod Com Tam
description: A delicious and tender Vietnamese dish featuring salt cod, served with com tam, a popular Vietnamese street food, this recipe is onion and garlic free, making it suitable for those with sensitivities, and is a great option for a solo dinner
instructions: Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the cornstarch, sugar, and black pepper.
Add the salt cod to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the cornstarch mixture.
Double fry the salt cod by first frying at 325 degrees Fahrenheit for 3 minutes, then increasing the temperature to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and frying for an additional 2 minutes.
Remove the salt cod from the oil and place on a paper towel lined plate to drain excess oil.
In a small bowl, whisk together the fish sauce and lime juice.
To assemble the com tam, place a scoop of cooked rice on a plate, then add a piece of the fried salt cod, a spoonful of the fish sauce mixture, and a sprinkle of chopped scallions and diced tomato.
Garnish with additional scallions and tomato if desired.
